questions.question: Veer Gatha 5.0 is a joint initiative of which ministries?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Culture and Ministry of Education

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Ministry of Education

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Home Affairs and Ministry of Tourism

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Defence and Ministry of Education

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Defence and Ministry of Education

questions.explanation: Minister of State for Defence Sanjay Seth and Minister of State for Education Jayant Chaudhary felicitated winners at the Veer Gatha 5.0 awards ceremony in New Delhi. Veer Gatha 5.0 is a joint initiative of the Ministries of Defence and Education, celebrating the courage of Gallantry awardees. This year, 100 winners were selected from 91 schools across 18 countries under three categories. Each winner received a cash prize of ₹10,000 and an opportunity to witness the Republic Day Parade 2026 at Kartavya Path as special guests. Veer Gatha was launched in 2021 as part of Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav.

questions.question: Bactrian camels, which recently participated in the Republic Day Parade 2026, are mainly found in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Double-humped Bactrian camels from Ladakh, named Galwan and Nubra, participated in the Republic Day Parade on Kartavya Path for the first time. They represents India’s ancient Silk Route heritage, which connected India with Central Asia, Mongolia and China for over 6,000 years. In India, it is found only in Ladakh’s Nubra Valley, making the parade appearance nationally significant. Globally, it occurs in Mongolia, China, Kazakhstan, and Central Asia.

questions.question: Which institution launched Sampoornata Abhiyan 2.0?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog launched ‘Sampoornata Abhiyan 2.0’, a 3-month campaign from 28th January to 14th April 2026, to saturate key indicators in Aspirational Districts and Blocks. The campaign builds on Sampoornata Abhiyan 2024, targeting 112 Aspirational Districts and 513 Aspirational Blocks under the Aspirational Districts and Blocks Programme. Districts/Blocks will develop 3-month action plans, track monthly progress, conduct awareness campaigns, and perform concurrent monitoring visits. NITI Aayog, Central Ministries, States, and UTs will coordinate for planning, capacity building, and sustainable service delivery.

questions.question: The National Career Service (NCS) Portal is an initiative under which ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: The National Career Service (NCS) Portal is a digital platform providing career services such as job listings, job fairs, career counselling, vocational guidance, and skill development information. It is an initiative under the Ministry of Labour and Employment It aims to connect jobseekers and employers, offering decent employment opportunities and career development assistance. As of 20 January 2026, more than 2.23 crore jobseekers have been shortlisted. The portal offers online employability and digital skill enhancement programmes.

questions.question: White Revolution 2.0 has been launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying

questions.explanation: Union Minister for Home and Cooperation Amit Shah informed in Lok Sabha that the Centre launched White Revolution 2.0 under Ministry of Cooperation. The initiative aims to expand dairy cooperatives, generate employment and strengthen women’s empowerment. Milk procurement by dairy cooperatives is targeted to increase by 50% to 1,007 lakh kilogram per day by 2028–29. Around 75,000 new Dairy Cooperative Societies (DCS) will be established and 46,422 existing DCS will be strengthened. The scheme will reduce post-harvest losses, improve income and enhance nutritional security.

questions.question: National Medicinal Plants Board (NMPB) function under which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

questions.explanation: The National Medicinal Plants Board (NMPB), under the Ministry of AYUSH, organised a one-day Chintan Shivir at Vigyan Bhawan. National Medicinal Plants Board (NMPB) was established in 2000 by the Government of India. It functions under the Ministry of AYUSH, which covers Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha & Homoeopathy. The board’s mandate is to coordinate between ministries, departments, and organizations for the growth of the medicinal plants sector at Central, State, and international levels.

questions.question: The Urban Challenge Fund is a new Centrally Sponsored Scheme under which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

questions.explanation: Recently, the Union Cabinet chaired by the Prime Minister approved the launch of the Urban Challenge Fund (UCF) to strengthen urban development in India. The Urban Challenge Fund is a new Centrally Sponsored Scheme under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. The Fund aims to promote resilient, productive, inclusive, and climate-responsive cities, positioning urban centres as key drivers of India’s next phase of economic growth. Under the scheme, at least 50 per cent of project financing must be mobilised from market sources such as municipal bonds, bank loans, and Public–Private Partnerships (PPPs), while the remaining share can be contributed by States, Union Territories, Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), or other sources.

questions.question: The Karnataka Bharat Gaurav Train operates under which policy of Indian Railways?
questions.explanation: Union Minister of State V. Somanna launched the coffee table book “Karnataka Bharat Gaurav Train: Journey of a Thousand Shrines” at Rail Bhawan on 23 February 2026. The book highlights the Karnataka Bharat Gaurav Train as a major cultural and spiritual tourism initiative of Indian Railways. The initiative was conceptualised by Dr. Anup Dayanand, Indian Railway Traffic Service (Retired). The Karnataka Bharat Gaurav Train is a flagship pilgrimage tourism initiative launched under the Bharat Gaurav Policy of Indian Railways. The initiative transforms Indian Railways from being only a transport provider into a promoter of tourism, culture and spiritual connectivity.

questions.question: Where was Chungthang–Lachen axis and Bailey Suspension Taram Chu Bridge inaugurated recently?
questions.explanation: Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth inaugurated the Chungthang–Lachen axis and the 400 ft Bailey Suspension Taram Chu Bridge in North Sikkim, restored by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO). Restoration follows major disasters: cloud bursts (May–June 2025), Cyclone Remal (June 2024), and Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (October 2023). Under Project Swastik, BRO cleared 96 landslides, constructed four major bridges, and repaired two others to restore Lines of Communication. The engineering team completed 8 km of fresh formation cutting and created diversions to navigate unstable slopes and sinking zones.

questions.question: A wild banana Musa sikkimensis, that was recently seen in news, is native to which region?
questions.explanation: Researchers at Nagaland University studied the genetic diversity of Musa sikkimensis, a wild banana native to the Eastern Himalayas and Northeast India. Known as Darjeeling or Sikkim banana, it is a wild seeded species with traits for disease resistance, environmental stress tolerance, and climate adaptability, crucial for future breeding. Though not widely cultivated for fruit, it strengthens crop resilience, food security, and sustainable agriculture. Nagaland, part of the Indo Burma biodiversity hotspot, hosts diverse indigenous banana genotypes at risk due to deforestation and human activity.
